contentBlocks.original:
Narayana Kavacham
अथ श्रीनारायणकवचम् ।
राजोवाच ।
यया गुप्तः सहस्राक्षः सवाहान्रिपुसैनिकान् ।
क्रीडन्निव विनिर्जित्य त्रिलोक्या बुभुजे श्रियम् ॥ १॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha śrīnārāyaṇakavacam .
rājovāca .
yayā guptaḥ sahasrākṣaḥ savāhānripusainikān .
krīḍanniva vinirjitya trilokyā bubhuje śriyam .. 1..
contentBlocks.translation:
Now begins the Narayana Kavacham.
The king Parikshit said:
By which protection, the thousand-eyed Lord Vishnu defeated his enemies and enjoyed the wealth of the three worlds as if in play.
contentBlocks.meanings: अथ (atha) – now; श्रीनारायणकवचम् (śrīnārāyaṇakavacam) – Narayana Kavacham (śrī:glorious | nārāyaṇa:Narayana | kavacam:armor); राजोवाच (rājovāca) – the king said (rājā:king | uvāca:said); यया (yayā) – by which; गुप्तः (guptaḥ) – protected; सहस्राक्षः (sahasrākṣaḥ) – thousand-eyed (sahasra:thousand | akṣaḥ:eyes); सवाहान् (savāhān) – with vehicles (sa:with | vāhān:vehicles); रिपुसैनिकान् (ripusainikān) – enemy soldiers (ripu:enemy | sainikān:soldiers); क्रीडन्निव (krīḍanniva) – as if playing (krīḍan:playing | iva:as if); विनिर्जित्य (vinirjitya) – having conquered; त्रिलोक्या (trilokyā) – of the three worlds (tri:three | lokyā:worlds); बुभुजे (bubhuje) – enjoyed; श्रियम् (śriyam) – wealth;

contentBlocks.original:
भगवंस्तन्ममाख्याहि वर्म नारायणात्मकम् ।
यथाऽऽततायिनः शत्रून् येन गुप्तोऽजयन्मृधे ॥ २॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhagavaṃs tan mamākhyāhi varma nārāyaṇātmakam .
yathā''tatāyinaḥ śatrūn yena gupto'jayanmṛdhe .. 2..
contentBlocks.translation:
O Bhagavan, please tell me the armor that is the essence of Narayana,
By which, protected, the enemies of the one who is steadfast were defeated in battle.
contentBlocks.meanings: भगवन् (bhagavan) – O Bhagavan; तत् (tat) – that; मम (mama) – my; आख्याहि (ākhyāhi) – please tell; वर्म (varma) – armor; नारायणात्मकम् (nārāyaṇātmakam) – essence of Narayana (nārāyaṇa:Narayana | ātmakam:essence of); यथा (yathā) – by which; आततायिनः (ātatāyinaḥ) – enemies; शत्रून् (śatrūn) – enemies; येन (yena) – by which; गुप्तः (guptaḥ) – protected; अजयत् (ajayat) – defeated; मृधे (mṛdhe) – in battle;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीशुक उवाच ।
वृतः पुरोहितस्त्वाष्ट्रो महेन्द्रायानुपृच्छते ।
नारायणाख्यं वर्माह तदिहैकमनाः श‍ृणु ॥ ३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśuka uvāca .
vṛtaḥ purohitastvāṣṭro mahendrāyānupṛcchate .
nārāyaṇākhyaṃ varmāha tadihaikamanāḥ śṛṇu .. 3..
contentBlocks.translation: Sri Sukadeva said: King Indra, the leader of the demigods, inquired about the armor known as Narayana-kavaca from Visvarupa, who was engaged by the demigods as their priest. Please hear Visvarupa's reply with great attention.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीशुक (śrīśuka) – Sri Sukadeva (śrī:Sri | śuka:Sukadeva); उवाच (uvāca) – said; वृतः (vṛtaḥ) – engaged; पुरोहितः (purohitaḥ) – priest; त्वाष्ट्रः (tvāṣṭraḥ) – son of Tvashta; महेन्द्राय (mahendrāya) – to Mahendra; अनुपृच्छते (anupṛcchate) – inquired; नारायणाख्यम् (nārāyaṇākhyam) – known as Narayana (nārāyaṇa:Narayana | ākhyam:known as); वर्म (varma) – armor; आह (āha) – said; तत् (tat) – that; इह (iha) – here; एकमनाः (ekamanāḥ) – with great attention (eka:one | manāḥ:mind); शृणु (śṛṇu) – hear;

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वरूप उवाच ।
धौताङ्घ्रिपाणिराचम्य सपवित्र उदङ्मुखः ।
कृतस्वाङ्गकरन्यासो मन्त्राभ्यां वाग्यतः शुचिः ॥ ४॥

नारायणमयं वर्म सन्नह्येद्भय आगते ।
पादयोर्जानुनोरूर्वोरुदरे हृद्यथोरसि ॥ ५॥

मुखे शिरस्यानुपूर्व्यादोङ्कारादीनि विन्यसेत् ।
ॐ नमो नारायणायेति विपर्ययमथापि वा ॥ ६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvarūpa uvāca .
dhautāṅghripāṇirācamya sapavitra udaṅmukhaḥ .
kṛtasvāṅgakaranyāso mantrābhyāṃ vāgyataḥ śuciḥ .. 4..

nārāyaṇamayaṃ varma sannahyedbhaya āgate .
pādayorjānunorūrvorudare hṛdyathorasi .. 5..

mukhe śirasyānupūrvyād oṅkārādīni vinyaset .
oṃ namo nārāyaṇāyeti viparyayamathāpi vā .. 6..
contentBlocks.translation: Visvarupa said: If some form of fear arrives, one should first wash his hands and legs clean and then perform acamana by chanting this mantra: om apavitrah pavitro va sarvavastham gato 'pi va. yah smaret pundarikaksam sa bahyabhyantarah sucih. sri-visnu sri-visnu sri-visnu. Then one should touch kusa grass and sit gravely and silently, facing north. When completely purified, one should touch the mantra composed of eight syllables to the eight parts of his body and touch the mantra composed of twelve syllables to his hands. Thus, in the following manner, he should bind himself with the Narayana coat of armor. First, while chanting the mantra composed of eight syllables [om namo narayanaya], beginning with the pranava, the syllable om, one should touch his hands to eight parts of his body, starting with the two feet and progressing systematically to the knees, thighs, abdomen, heart, chest, mouth and head. Then one should chant the mantra in reverse, beginning from the last syllable [ya], while touching the parts of his body in the reverse order. These two processes are known as utpatti-nyasa and samhara-nyasa respectively.
contentBlocks.meanings: विश्वरूप (viśvarūpa) – Visvarupa; उवाच (uvāca) – said; धौताङ्घ्रिपाणिः (dhautāṅghripāṇiḥ) – with washed hands and feet (dhauta:washed | aṅghri:feet | pāṇiḥ:hands); आचम्य (ācamya) – having sipped water; सपवित्र (sapavitra) – with purity; उदङ्मुखः (udaṅmukhaḥ) – facing north; कृतस्वाङ्गकरन्यासः (kṛtasvāṅgakaranyāsaḥ) – having performed body gestures (kṛta:performed | sva:own | aṅga:body | kara:hand | nyāsaḥ:gestures); मन्त्राभ्याम् (mantrābhyām) – with two mantras; वाग्यतः (vāgyataḥ) – silent; शुचिः (śuciḥ) – pure; नारायणमयम् (nārāyaṇamayam) – composed of Narayana (nārāyaṇa:Narayana | maya:composed of); वर्म (varma) – armor; सन्नह्येत् (sannahyet) – should be worn; भये (bhaye) – in fear; आगते (āgate) – when arrived; पादयोः (pādayoḥ) – on the feet; जानुनोः (jānunoḥ) – on the knees; ऊर्वोः (ūrvoḥ) – on the thighs; उदरे (udare) – on the abdomen; हृदि (hṛdi) – on the heart; अथ (atha) – then; उरसि (urasi) – on the chest; मुखे (mukhe) – on the mouth; शिरसि (śirasi) – on the head; अनुपूर्व्यात् (anupūrvyāt) – in sequence; ओङ्कारादीनि (oṅkārādīni) – beginning with Om (oṅkāra:Om | ādi:beginning | ini:with); विन्यसेत् (vinyaset) – should place; ॐ (oṃ) – Om; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; नारायणाय (nārāyaṇāya) – to Narayana; इति (iti) – thus; विपर्ययम् (viparyayam) – in reverse; अथ (atha) – or; अपि (api) – also; वा (vā) – or;

contentBlocks.original:
करन्यासं ततः कुर्याद्द्वादशाक्षरविद्यया ।
प्रणवादियकारान्तमङ्गुल्यङ्गुष्ठ पर्वसु ॥ ७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karanyāsaṃ tataḥ kuryāddvādaśākṣaravidyayā .
Praṇavādiyakārāntam aṅgulyaṅguṣṭha parvasu .. 7..
contentBlocks.translation: Then one should chant the mantra composed of twelve syllables [om namo bhagavate vasudevaya]. Preceding each syllable by the omkara, one should place the syllables of the mantra on the tips of his fingers, beginning with the index finger of the right hand and concluding with the index finger of the left. The four remaining syllables should be placed on the joints of the thumbs.
contentBlocks.meanings: करन्यासं (karanyāsaṃ) – finger placement (kara:hand | nyāsaṃ:placement); ततः (tataḥ) – then; कुर्यात् (kuryāt) – should perform; द्वादशाक्षरविद्यया (dvādaśākṣaravidyayā) – with twelve-syllable mantra (dvādaśa:twelve | akṣara:syllable | vidyayā:with mantra); । (.) – None; प्रणवादियकारान्तम् (praṇavādiyakārāntam) – beginning with om, ending with ya (praṇava:om | ādi:beginning | yakāra:ya syllable | antam:ending); अङ्गुल्यङ्गुष्ठ (aṅgulyaṅguṣṭha) – fingers and thumbs (aṅguli:finger | aṅguṣṭha:thumb); पर्वसु (parvasu) – on the joints; ॥ (||) – None; ७ (7) – None;

contentBlocks.original:
न्यसेद्धृदय ओङ्कारं विकारमनु मूर्धनि ।
षकारं तु भ्रुवोर्मध्ये णकारं शिखया दिशेत् ॥ ८॥

वेकारं नेत्रयोर्युञ्ज्यान्नकारं सर्वसन्धिषु ।
मकारमस्त्रमुद्दिश्य मन्त्रमूर्तिर्भवेद्बुधः ॥ ९॥

सविसर्गं फडन्तं तत् सर्वदिक्षु विनिर्दिशेत् ।
ॐ विष्णवे नम इति ॥ १०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nyaseddhṛdaya oṅkāraṃ vikāramanu mūrdhani .
ṣakāraṃ tu bhruvormadhye ṇakāraṃ śikhayā diśet .. 8..

vekāraṃ netrayoryuñjyānnakāraṃ sarvasandhiṣu .
makāramastramuddiśya mantramūrtirbhavedbudhaḥ .. 9..

savisargaṃ phaḍantaṃ tat sarvadikṣu vinirdiśet .
oṃ viṣṇave nama iti .. 10..
contentBlocks.translation: One must then chant the mantra of six syllables [om visnave namah]. One should place the syllable "om" on his heart, the syllable "vi" on the top of his head, the syllable "sa" between his eyebrows, the syllable "na" on his tuft of hair [sikha], and the syllable "ve" between his eyes. The chanter of the mantra should then place the syllable "na" on all the joints of his body and meditate on the syllable "ma" as being a weapon. He should thus become the perfect personification of the mantra. Thereafter, adding visarga to the final syllable "ma," he should chant the mantra "mah astraya phat" in all directions, beginning from the east. In this way, all directions will be bound by the protective armor of the mantra.
contentBlocks.meanings: न्यसेत् (nyaset) – should place; हृदय (hṛdaya) – heart; ओङ्कारं (oṅkāraṃ) – syllable 'om'; विकारम् (vikāram) – syllable 'vi'; अनु (anu) – after; मूर्धनि (mūrdhani) – on the head; षकारं (ṣakāraṃ) – syllable 'ṣa'; तु (tu) – but; भ्रुवोः (bhruvoḥ) – of the eyebrows; मध्ये (madhye) – between; णकारं (ṇakāraṃ) – syllable 'ṇa'; शिखया (śikhayā) – with the tuft; दिशेत् (diśet) – should direct; वेकारं (vekāraṃ) – syllable 've'; नेत्रयोः (netrayoḥ) – of the eyes; युञ्ज्यात् (yuñjyāt) – should apply; णकारं (ṇakāraṃ) – syllable 'ṇa'; सर्वसन्धिषु (sarvasandhiṣu) – in all joints (sarva:all | sandhiṣu:in the joints); मकारम् (makāram) – syllable 'ma'; अस्त्रम् (astram) – weapon; उद्दिश्य (uddiśya) – having aimed; मन्त्रमूर्तिः (mantramūrtiḥ) – personification of the mantra (mantra:mantra | mūrtiḥ:personification); भवेत् (bhavet) – should become; बुधः (budhaḥ) – wise person; सविसर्गं (savisargaṃ) – with visarga; फडन्तं (phaḍantaṃ) – ending with 'phaṭ'; तत् (tat) – that; सर्वदिक्षु (sarvadikṣu) – in all directions (sarva:all | dikṣu:in directions); विनिर्दिशेत् (vinirdiśet) – should direct; ॐ (oṃ) – Om; विष्णवे (viṣṇave) – to Vishnu; नम (nama) – salutations; इति (iti) – thus;

contentBlocks.original:
आत्मानं परमं ध्यायेद्ध्येयं षट्शक्तिभिर्युतम् ।
विद्यातेजस्तपोमूर्तिमिमं मन्त्रमुदाहरेत् ॥ ११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ātmānaṃ paramaṃ dhyāyeddhyeyaṃ ṣaṭśaktibhiryutam .
vidyātejastapomūrtimimaṃ mantramudāharet .. 11..
contentBlocks.translation: After finishing this chanting, one should think himself qualitatively one with the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is full in six opulences and is worthy to be meditated upon. Then one should chant the following protective prayer to Lord Narayana, the Narayana-kavaca.
contentBlocks.meanings: आत्मानं (ātmānaṃ) – self; परमं (paramaṃ) – supreme; ध्यायेत् (dhyāyet) – should meditate; ध्येयं (dhyeyaṃ) – worthy of meditation; षट्शक्तिभिः (ṣaṭśaktibhiḥ) – with six opulences (ṣaṭ:six | śaktibhiḥ:with opulences); युतम् (yutam) – endowed; विद्या (vidyā) – knowledge; तेजः (tejaḥ) – vigor; तपः (tapaḥ) – austerity; मूर्तिम् (mūrtim) – form; इमम् (imam) – this; मन्त्रम् (mantram) – mantra; उदाहरेत् (udāharet) – should chant;

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ हरिर्विदध्यान्मम सर्वरक्षां
न्यस्ताङ्घ्रिपद्मः पतगेन्द्रपृष्ठे ।
दरारिचर्मासिगदेषुचाप-
पाशान्दधानोऽष्टगुणोऽष्टबाहुः ॥ १२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ harirvidadhyānmama sarvarakṣāṃ
nyastāṅghripadmaḥ patagendrapṛṣṭhe .
darāricarmāsigadeṣucāpa-
pāśāndadhāno'ṣṭaguṇo'ṣṭabāhuḥ .. 12..
contentBlocks.translation: The Supreme Lord, who sits on the back of the bird Garuda, touching him with His lotus feet, holds eight weapons--the conchshell, disc, shield, sword, club, arrows, bow and ropes. May that Supreme Personality of Godhead protect me at all times with His eight arms. He is all-powerful because He fully possesses the eight mystic powers [anima, laghima, etc.].
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – sacred syllable; हरिः (hariḥ) – Hari (Vishnu); विध्यात् (vidhyāt) – may protect; मम (mama) – my; सर्वरक्षाम् (sarvarakṣām) – all protection (sarva:all | rakṣām:protection); न्यस्ताङ्घ्रिपद्मः (nyastāṅghripadmaḥ) – with lotus feet placed (nyasta:placed | aṅghri:feet | padmaḥ:lotus); पतगेन्द्रपृष्ठे (patagendrapṛṣṭhe) – on Garuda's back (pataga:bird | indra:king | pṛṣṭhe:on the back); दरारिचर्मासिगदेषुचापपाशान् (darāricarmāsigadeṣucāpapāśān) – conch, disc, shield, sword, club, arrows, bow, ropes (darāri:conch, disc | carma:shield | asi:sword | gada:club | iṣu:arrows | cāpa:bow | pāśān:ropes); दधानः (dadhānaḥ) – holding; अष्टगुणः (aṣṭaguṇaḥ) – with eight qualities (aṣṭa:eight | guṇaḥ:qualities); अष्टबाहुः (aṣṭabāhuḥ) – eight-armed (aṣṭa:eight | bāhuḥ:armed);

contentBlocks.original:
जलेषु मां रक्षतु मत्स्यमूर्ति-
र्यादोगणेभ्यो वरुणस्य पाशात् ।
स्थलेषु मायावटुवामनोऽव्यात्
त्रिविक्रमः खेऽवतु विश्वरूपः ॥ १३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaleṣu māṃ rakṣatu matsyamūrti-
ryādogaṇebhyo varuṇasya pāśāt .
sthaleṣu māyāvaṭuvāmano'vyāt
trivikramaḥ khe'vatu viśvarūpaḥ .. 13..
contentBlocks.translation: May the Lord, who assumes the body of a great fish, protect me in the water from the fierce animals that are associates of the demigod Varuna. By expanding His illusory energy, the Lord assumed the form of the dwarf Vamana. May Vamana protect me on the land. Since the gigantic form of the Lord, Visvarupa, conquers the three worlds, may He protect me in the sky.
contentBlocks.meanings: जलेषु (jaleṣu) – in the waters; मां (māṃ) – me; रक्षतु (rakṣatu) – may protect; मत्स्यमूर्ति (matsyamūrtiḥ) – fish form (matsya:fish | mūrti:form); यादोगणेभ्यः (yādogaṇebhyaḥ) – from fierce animals (yāda:fierce | gaṇebhyaḥ:from groups); वरुणस्य (varuṇasya) – of Varuna; पाशात् (pāśāt) – from the noose; स्थलेषु (sthaleṣu) – on the land; मायावटुवामनः (māyāvaṭuvāmanaḥ) – illusory dwarf Vamana (māyā:illusory | vaṭu:dwarf | vāmanaḥ:Vamana); अव्यात् (avyāt) – may protect; त्रिविक्रमः (trivikramaḥ) – Trivikrama; खे (khe) – in the sky; अवतु (avatu) – may protect; विश्वरूपः (viśvarūpaḥ) – Visvarupa;

contentBlocks.original:
दुर्गेष्वटव्याजिमुखादिषु प्रभुः
पायान्नृसिंहोऽसुरयूथपारिः ।
विमुञ्चतो यस्य महाट्टहासं
दिशो विनेदुर्न्यपतंश्च गर्भाः ॥ १४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
durgeṣvaṭavyājimukhādiṣu prabhuḥ
pāyānnṛsiṃho'surayūthapāriḥ .
vimuñcato yasya mahāṭṭahāsaṃ
diśo vinedurnyapataṃśca garbhāḥ .. 14..
contentBlocks.translation: May Lord Narsimhadeva, who appeared as the enemy of Hiranyakasipu, protect me in all directions. His loud laughing vibrated in all directions and caused the pregnant wives of the asuras to have miscarriages. May that Lord be kind enough to protect me in difficult places like the forest and battlefront.
contentBlocks.meanings: दुर्गेषु (durgeṣu) – in difficult places; अटव्याजिमुखादिषु (aṭavyājimukhādiṣu) – like forest and battlefront (aṭavi:forest | ājimukha:battlefront | ādiṣu:and others); प्रभुः (prabhuḥ) – Lord; पायात् (pāyāt) – may protect; नृसिंहः (nṛsiṃhaḥ) – Narasimha; असुरयूथपारिः (asurayūthapāriḥ) – enemy of asura leaders (asura:demon | yūtha:group | pāriḥ:enemy); विमुञ्चतः (vimuñcataḥ) – emitting; यस्य (yasya) – whose; महाट्टहासं (mahāṭṭahāsaṃ) – loud laughter (mahā:great | aṭṭahāsaṃ:laughter); दिशः (diśaḥ) – directions; विनेदुः (vineduḥ) – resounded; न्यपतन् (nyapatan) – fell down; च (ca) – and; गर्भाः (garbhāḥ) – embryos;

contentBlocks.original:
रक्षत्वसौ माध्वनि यज्ञकल्पः
स्वदंष्ट्रयोन्नीतधरो वराहः ।
रामोऽद्रिकूटेष्वथ विप्रवासे
सलक्ष्मणोऽव्याद्भरताग्रजोऽस्मान् ॥ १५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rakṣatvasau mādhvani yajñakalpaḥ
svadaṃṣṭrayonnītadharo varāhaḥ .
rāmo'drikūṭeṣvatha vipravāse
salakṣmaṇo'vyādbharatāgrajo'smān .. 15..
contentBlocks.translation: The Supreme indestructible Lord is ascertained through the performance of ritualistic sacrifices and is therefore known as Yajnesvara. In His incarnation as Lord Boar, He raised the planet earth from the water at the bottom of the universe and kept it on His pointed tusks. May that Lord protect me from rogues on the street. May Parasurama protect me on the tops of mountains, and may the elder brother of Bharata, Lord Ramacandra, along with His brother Laksmana, protect me in foreign lands.
contentBlocks.meanings: रक्षतु (rakṣatu) – may protect; असौ (asau) – that; माध्वनि (mādhvani) – on the street; यज्ञकल्पः (yajñakalpaḥ) – like a sacrifice (yajña:sacrifice | kalpaḥ:like); स्वदंष्ट्रया (svadaṃṣṭrayā) – with His tusk (sva:His | daṃṣṭrayā:with tusk); उन्नीतधरः (unnītadharaḥ) – raised the earth (unnīta:raised | dharaḥ:earth); वराहः (varāhaḥ) – Boar; रामः (rāmaḥ) – Rama; अद्रिकूटेषु (adrikūṭeṣu) – on mountain tops (adri:mountain | kūṭeṣu:on tops); अथ (atha) – and; विप्रवासे (vipravāse) – in foreign lands (vipra:foreign | vāse:lands); सलक्ष्मणः (salakṣmaṇaḥ) – with Lakshmana (sa:with | lakṣmaṇaḥ:Lakshmana); अव्यात् (avyāt) – may protect; भरताग्रजः (bharatāgrajaḥ) – elder brother of Bharata (bharata:Bharata | agrajaḥ:elder brother); अस्मान् (asmān) – us;

contentBlocks.original:
मामुग्रधर्मादखिलात्प्रमादा-
न्नारायणः पातु नरश्च हासात् ।
दत्तस्त्वयोगादथ योगनाथः
पायाद्गुणेशः कपिलः कर्मबन्धात् ॥ १६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māmugradharmādakhilātpramādā-
nnārāyaṇaḥ pātu naraśca hāsāt .
dattastvayogādatha yoganāthaḥ
pāyādguṇeśaḥ kapilaḥ karmabandhāt .. 16..
contentBlocks.translation: May Lord Narayana protect me from unnecessarily following false religious systems and falling from my duties due to madness. May the Lord in His appearance as Nara protect me from unnecessary pride. May Lord Dattatreya, the master of all mystic power, protect me from falling while performing bhakti-yoga, and may Lord Kapila, the master of all good qualities, protect me from the material bondage of fruitive activities.
contentBlocks.meanings: माम् (mām) – me; उग्रधर्मात् (ugradharmāt) – from false religious systems (ugra:fierce, false | dharmāt:from religious systems); अखिलात् (akhilāt) – from all; प्रमादनात् (pramādān) – from madness; नारायणः (nārāyaṇaḥ) – Lord Narayana; पातु (pātu) – may protect; नरः (naraḥ) – Lord Nara; च (ca) – and; हासात् (hāsāt) – from pride; दत्तः (dattaḥ) – Lord Dattatreya; तु (tu) – but; अयोगात् (ayogāt) – from falling in yoga (a:not | yogāt:from yoga); अथ (atha) – then; योगनाथः (yoganāthaḥ) – master of yoga (yoga:yoga | nāthaḥ:master); पायात् (pāyāt) – may protect; गुणेशः (guṇeśaḥ) – master of qualities (guṇa:qualities | īśaḥ:master); कपिलः (kapilaḥ) – Lord Kapila; कर्मबन्धात् (karmabandhāt) – from bondage of actions (karma:actions | bandhāt:from bondage);

contentBlocks.original:
सनत्कुमारोऽवतु कामदेवा-
द्धयशीर्षा मां पथि देवहेलनात् ।
देवर्षिवर्यः पुरुषार्चनान्तरात्
कूर्मो हरिर्मां निरयादशेषात् ॥ १७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sanatkumāro'vatu kāmadevā-
ddhayaśīrṣā māṃ pathi devahelanāt .
devarṣivaryaḥ puruṣārcanāntarāt
kūrmo harirmāṃ nirayādaśeṣāt .. 17..
contentBlocks.translation: May Sanat-kumara protect me from lusty desires. As I begin some auspicious activity, may Lord Hayagriva protect me from being an offender by neglecting to offer respectful obeisances to the Supreme Lord. May Devarsi Narada protect me from committing offenses in worshiping the Deity, and may Lord Kurma, the tortoise, protect me from falling to the unlimited hellish planets.
contentBlocks.meanings: सनत्कुमारः (sanatkumāraḥ) – Sanat-kumara; अवतु (avatu) – may protect; कामदेवात् (kāmadevāt) – from lusty desires (kāma:desire | devāt:from the god); हयशीर्षः (hayaśīrṣaḥ) – Hayagriva; माम् (mām) – me; पथि (pathi) – on the path; देवहेलनात् (devahelanāt) – from neglecting the deity (deva:deity | helanāt:from neglecting); देवर्षिवर्यः (devarṣivaryaḥ) – best of divine sages (deva:divine | ṛṣi:sage | varyaḥ:best); पुरुषार्चनान्तरात् (puruṣārcanāntarāt) – from worshiping the deity (puruṣa:deity | arcana:worship | antarāt:from within); कूर्मः (kūrmaḥ) – Kurma; हरिः (hariḥ) – Hari; माम् (mām) – me; निरयात् (nirayāt) – from hell; अशेषात् (aśeṣāt) – unlimited;

contentBlocks.original:
धन्वन्तरिर्भगवान्पात्वपथ्या-
द्द्वन्द्वाद्भयादृषभो निर्जितात्मा ।
यज्ञश्च लोकादवताञ्ज्जनान्ता-
द्बलो गणात्क्रोधवशादहीन्द्रः ॥ १८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhanvantarirbhagavānpātvapathyā-
ddvandvādbhayādṛṣabho nirjitātmā .
yajñaśca lokādavatāñjjanāntā-
dbalo gaṇātkrodhavaśādahīndraḥ .. 18..
contentBlocks.translation: May the Supreme Personality of Godhead in His incarnation as Dhanvantari relieve me from undesirable eatables and protect me from physical illness. May Lord Rsabhadeva, who conquered His inner and outer senses, protect me from fear produced by the duality of heat and cold. May Yajna protect me from defamation and harm from the populace, and may Lord Balarama as Sesa protect me from envious serpents.
contentBlocks.meanings: धन्वन्तरिः (dhanvantarir) – Dhanvantari; भगवान् (bhagavān) – Supreme Personality of Godhead; पातु (pātu) – may protect; अपथ्यात् (apathyāt) – from undesirable eatables; द्वन्द्वात् (dvandvāt) – from duality; भयात् (bhayāt) – from fear; ऋषभः (ṛṣabhaḥ) – Rsabhadeva; निर्जितात्मा (nirjitātmā) – conquered inner and outer senses (nirjita:conquered | ātmā:inner and outer senses); यज्ञः (yajñaḥ) – Yajna; च (ca) – and; लोकात् (lokāt) – from the populace; अवतात् (avatāt) – may protect; जनान्तात् (janāntāt) – from defamation; बलः (balaḥ) – Balarama; गणात् (gaṇāt) – from envious serpents; क्रोधवशात् (krodhavaśāt) – from anger (krodha:anger | vaśāt:from); अहीन्द्रः (ahīndraḥ) – king of serpents;

contentBlocks.original:
द्वैपायनो भगवानप्रबोधा-
द्बुद्धस्तु पाखण्डगणप्रमादात् ।
कल्किः कलेः कालमलात्प्रपातु
धर्मावनायोरुकृतावतारः ॥ १९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dvaipāyano bhagavānaprabodhā-
dbuddhastu pākhaṇḍagaṇapramādāt .
kalkiḥ kaleḥ kālamalātprapātu
dharmāvanāyorukṛtāvatāraḥ .. 19..
contentBlocks.translation: May the Personality of Godhead in His incarnation as Vyasadeva protect me from all kinds of ignorance resulting from the absence of Vedic knowledge. May Lord Buddhadeva protect me from activities opposed to Vedic principles and from laziness that causes one to madly forget the Vedic principles of knowledge and ritualistic action. May Kalkideva, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who appeared as an incarnation to protect religious principles, protect me from the dirt of the age of Kali.
contentBlocks.meanings: द्वैपायनः (dvaipāyanaḥ) – Vyasa; भगवान् (bhagavān) – the Lord; प्रबोधात् (prabodhāt) – from ignorance; बुद्धः (buddhaḥ) – Buddha; तु (tu) – but; पाखण्डगणप्रमादात् (pākhaṇḍagaṇapramādāt) – from heresy and negligence (pākhaṇḍa:heresy | gaṇa:group | pramādāt:from negligence); कल्किः (kalkiḥ) – Kalki; कलेः (kaleḥ) – of Kali; कालमलात् (kālamalāt) – from the dirt of time (kāla:time | malāt:from dirt); प्रपातु (prapātu) – may protect; धर्मावनाय (dharmāvanāya) – for protection of dharma (dharma:righteousness | avanāya:for protection); उरुकृतावतारः (urukṛtāvatāraḥ) – greatly incarnated (uru:great | kṛta:done | avatāraḥ:incarnation);

contentBlocks.original:
मां केशवो गदया प्रातरव्या-
द्गोविन्द आसङ्गवमात्तवेणुः ।
नारायणः प्राह्ण उदात्तशक्ति-
र्मध्यन्दिने विष्णुररीन्द्रपाणिः ॥ २०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māṃ keśavo gadayā prātaravyā-
dgovinda āsaṅgavamāttaveṇuḥ .
nārāyaṇaḥ prāhṇa udāttaśakti-
rmadhyandine viṣṇurarīndrapāṇiḥ .. 20..
contentBlocks.translation: May Lord Kesava protect me with His club in the first portion of the day, and may Govinda, who is always engaged in playing His flute, protect me in the second portion of the day. May Lord Narayana, who is equipped with all potencies, protect me in the third part of the day, and may Lord Visnu, who carries a disc to kill His enemies, protect me in the fourth part of the day.
contentBlocks.meanings: मां (māṃ) – me; केशवः (keśavaḥ) – Lord Kesava; गदया (gadayā) – with club; प्रातरव्यात् (prātaravyāt) – protect in the morning (prātaḥ:morning | avyāt:protect); गोविन्दः (govindaḥ) – Govinda; आसङ्गवम् (āsaṅgavam) – second part of day; आत्तवेणुः (āttaveṇuḥ) – holding flute (ātta:holding | veṇuḥ:flute); नारायणः (nārāyaṇaḥ) – Lord Narayana; प्राह्ण (prāhṇa) – third part of day; उदात्तशक्तिः (udāttaśaktiḥ) – equipped with potencies (udātta:equipped | śaktiḥ:potencies); मध्यन्दिने (madhyandine) – midday; विष्णुः (viṣṇuḥ) – Lord Visnu; अरीन्द्रपाणिः (arīndrapāṇiḥ) – enemy-slaying disc holder (arīndra:enemy-slaying | pāṇiḥ:disc holder);

contentBlocks.original:
देवोऽपराह्ने मधुहोग्रधन्वा
सायं त्रिधामावतु माधवो माम् ।
दोषे हृषीकेश उतार्धरात्रे
निशीथ एकोऽवतु पद्मनाभः ॥ २१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devo'parāhne madhuhogradhanvā
sāyaṃ tridhāmāvatu mādhavo mām .
doṣe hṛṣīkeśa utārdharātre
niśītha eko'vatu padmanābhaḥ .. 21..
contentBlocks.translation: May Lord Madhusudana, who carries a bow very fearful for the demons, protect me during the fifth part of the day. In the evening, may Lord Madhava, appearing as Brahma, Visnu and Mahesvara, protect me, and in the beginning of night may Lord Hrsikesa protect me. At the dead of night [in the second and third parts of night] may Lord Padmanabha alone protect me.
contentBlocks.meanings: देवः (devaḥ) – Lord; अपराह्ने (aparāhne) – in the afternoon; मधुहोग्रधन्वा (madhu-hogra-dhanvā) – Madhu's fierce-bowed (madhu:Madhu | ugra:fierce | dhanvā:bowed); सायं (sāyaṃ) – in the evening; त्रिधामावतु (tridhāmāvatu) – three-formed protect (tri:three | dhāma:form | avatu:protect); माधवः (mādhavaḥ) – Madhava; माम् (mām) – me; दोषे (doṣe) – at nightfall; हृषीकेशः (hṛṣīkeśaḥ) – Hrsikesa; उत (uta) – and; अर्धरात्रे (ardharātre) – at midnight; निशीथे (niśīthe) – at dead of night; एकः (ekaḥ) – alone; अवतु (avatu) – protect; पद्मनाभः (padmanābhaḥ) – Padmanabha;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीवत्सधामापररात्र ईशः
प्रत्यूष ईशोऽसिधरो जनार्दनः ।
दामोदरोऽव्यादनुसन्ध्यं प्रभाते
विश्वेश्वरो भगवान् कालमूर्तिः ॥ २२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīvatsadhāmāpararātra īśaḥ
pratyūṣa īśo'sidharo janārdanaḥ .
dāmodaro'vyādanusandhyaṃ prabhāte
viśveśvaro bhagavān kālamūrtiḥ .. 22..
contentBlocks.translation: May the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who bears the Srivatsa on His chest, protect me after midnight until the sky becomes pinkish. May Lord Janardana, who carries a sword in His hand, protect me at the end of night [during the last four ghatikas of night]. May Lord Damodara protect me in the early morning, and may Lord Visvesvara protect me during the junctions of day and night.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीवत्सधाम (śrīvatsadhāmā) – bearer of Śrīvatsa (śrīvatsa:Śrīvatsa | dhāmā:bearer); अपररात्र (apararātra) – after midnight (apara:after | rātra:night); ईशः (īśaḥ) – Lord; प्रत्यूष (pratyūṣa) – early morning; ईशः (īśaḥ) – Lord; असिधरः (asidharaḥ) – sword-bearer (asi:sword | dharaḥ:bearer); जनार्दनः (janārdanaḥ) – Janardana; दामोदरः (dāmodaraḥ) – Damodara; अव्यात् (avyāt) – may protect; अनुसन्ध्यं (anusandhyaṃ) – during junctions (anu:during | sandhyaṃ:junctions); प्रभाते (prabhāte) – in the morning; विश्वेश्वरः (viśveśvaraḥ) – Lord of the universe (viśva:universe | īśvaraḥ:Lord); भगवान् (bhagavān) – Supreme Personality; कालमूर्तिः (kālamūrtiḥ) – form of time (kāla:time | mūrtiḥ:form);

contentBlocks.original:
चक्रं युगान्तानलतिग्मनेमि
भ्रमत्समन्ताद्भगवत्प्रयुक्तम् ।
दन्दग्धि दन्दग्ध्यरिसैन्यमाशु
कक्षं यथा वातसखो हुताशः ॥ २३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
cakraṃ yugāntānalatigmanemi
bhramatsamantādbhagavatprayuktam .
dandagdhi dandagdhyarisainyamāśu
kakṣaṃ yathā vātasakho hutāśaḥ .. 23..
contentBlocks.translation: Set into motion by the Supreme Personality of Godhead and wandering in all the four directions, the disc of the Supreme Lord has sharp edges as destructive as the fire of devastation at the end of the millennium. As a blazing fire burns dry grass to ashes with the assistance of the breeze, may that Sudarsana cakra burn our enemies to ashes.
contentBlocks.meanings: चक्रं (cakraṃ) – disc; युगान्तानलतिग्मनेमि (yugāntānalatigmanemi) – sharp-edged like end-time fire (yuga:age | anta:end | anala:fire | tigmanemi:sharp-edged); भ्रमत्समन्तात् (bhramatsamantāt) – wandering all around (bhramat:wandering | samantāt:all around); भगवत्प्रयुक्तम् (bhagavatprayuktam) – set in motion by God (bhagavat:by God | prayuktam:set in motion); दन्दग्धि (dandagdhi) – burn; दन्दग्धि (dandagdhi) – burn; अरिसैन्यम् (arisainyam) – enemy army (ari:enemy | sainyam:army); आशु (āśu) – quickly; कक्षं (kakṣaṃ) – to ashes; यथा (yathā) – as; वातसखः (vātasakhaḥ) – wind's friend (vāta:wind | sakhaḥ:friend); हुताशः (hutāśaḥ) – fire;

contentBlocks.original:
गदेऽशनिस्पर्शनविस्फुलिङ्गे
निष्पिण्ढि निष्पिण्ढ्यजितप्रियासि ।
कूष्माण्डवैनायकयक्षरक्षो-
भूतग्रहांश्चूर्णय चूर्णयारीन् ॥ २४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gade'śanisparśanavisphuliṅge
niṣpiṇḍhi niṣpiṇḍhyajitapriyāsi .
kūṣmāṇḍavaināyakayakṣarakṣo-
bhūtagrahāṃścūrṇaya cūrṇayārīn .. 24..
contentBlocks.translation: O club in the hand of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, you produce sparks of fire as powerful as thunderbolts, and you are extremely dear to the Lord. I am also His servant. Therefore kindly help me pound to pieces the evil living beings known as Kusmandas, Vainayakas, Yaksas, Raksasas, Bhutas and Grahas. Please pulverize them.
contentBlocks.meanings: गदे (gade) – O club; अशनिस्पर्शनविस्फुलिङ्गे (aśanisparśanavisphuliṅge) – producing sparks like thunderbolts (aśani:thunderbolt | sparśana:touching | visphuliṅge:producing sparks); निष्पिण्ढि (niṣpiṇḍhi) – pound to pieces; निष्पिण्ढि (niṣpiṇḍhi) – pound to pieces; अजितप्रियासि (ajitapriyāsi) – dear to the unconquered (ajita:unconquered | priyāsi:dear to); कूष्माण्डवैनायकयक्षरक्षो (kūṣmāṇḍavaināyakayakṣarakṣo) – Kusmandas, Vainayakas, Yaksas, Raksasas (kūṣmāṇḍa:Kusmandas | vaināyaka:Vainayakas | yakṣa:Yaksas | rakṣaḥ:Raksasas); भूतग्रहान् (bhūtagrahān) – Bhutas and Grahas (bhūta:Bhutas | grahān:Grahas); चूर्णय (cūrṇaya) – pulverize; चूर्णय (cūrṇaya) – pulverize; अरिन् (arīn) – enemies;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वं यातुधान प्रमथप्रेतमातृ-
पिशाचविप्रग्रहघोरदृष्टीन् ।
दरेन्द्र विद्रावय कृष्णपूरितो
भीमस्वनोऽरेर्हृदयानि कम्पयन् ॥ २५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvaṃ yātudhāna pramathapretamātṛ-
piśāca vipragrahaghoradṛṣṭīn .
darendra vidrāvaya kṛṣṇapūrito
bhīmasvano'rerhṛdayāni kampayan .. 25..
contentBlocks.translation: O best of conchshells, O Pancajanya in the hands of the Lord, you are always filled with the breath of Lord Krsna. Therefore you create a fearful sound vibration that causes trembling in the hearts of enemies like the Raksasas, pramatha ghosts, Pretas, Matas, Pisacas and brahmana ghosts with fearful eyes.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; यातुधान (yātudhāna) – Raksasas; प्रमथप्रेतमातृ (pramathapretamātṛ) – pramatha ghosts, Pretas, Matas (pramatha:pramatha ghosts | preta:Pretas | mātṛ:Matas); पिशाचविप्रग्रहघोरदृष्टीन् (piśācavipragrahaghoradṛṣṭīn) – Pisacas and brahmana ghosts (piśāca:Pisacas | vipra:brahmana | graha:ghosts | ghora:fearful | dṛṣṭīn:eyes); दरेन्द्र (darendra) – O best of conchshells; विद्रावय (vidrāvaya) – cause to tremble; कृष्णपूरितो (kṛṣṇapūrito) – filled with Krsna's breath (kṛṣṇa:Krsna | pūrita:filled with); भीमस्वनः (bhīmasvanaḥ) – fearful sound (bhīma:fearful | svanaḥ:sound); अरेः (areḥ) – of the enemy; हृदयानि (hṛdayāni) – hearts; कम्पयन् (kampayan) – causing to tremble;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वं तिग्मधारासिवरारिसैन्य-
मीशप्रयुक्तो मम छिन्धि छिन्धि ।
चक्षूंषि चर्मञ्छतचन्द्र छादय
द्विषामघोनां हर पापचक्षुषाम् ॥ २६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvaṃ tigmadhārāsivarārisainya-
mīśaprayukto mama chindhi chindhi .
cakṣūṃṣi carmañchatacandra chādaya
dviṣāmaghonāṃ hara pāpacakṣuṣām .. 26..
contentBlocks.translation: O king of sharp-edged swords, you are engaged by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Please cut the soldiers of my enemies to pieces. Please cut them to pieces! O shield marked with a hundred brilliant moonlike circles, please cover the eyes of the sinful enemies. Pluck out their sinful eyes.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; तिग्मधारासिवरारिसैन्य (tigmadhārāsivarārisainya) – O king of sharp-edged swords (tigma:sharp | dhārā:edge | asi:sword | vara:best | ari:enemy | sainya:army); मीशप्रयुक्तः (mīśaprayuktaḥ) – engaged by the Supreme (mīśa:Supreme | prayuktaḥ:engaged); मम (mama) – my; छिन्धि (chindhi) – cut; छिन्धि (chindhi) – cut; चक्षूंषि (cakṣūṃṣi) – eyes; चर्मञ्छतचन्द्र (carmañchatacandra) – shield with hundred moons (carma:shield | śata:hundred | candra:moon); छादय (chādaya) – cover; द्विषाम् (dviṣām) – of enemies; अघोनाम् (aghonām) – sinful; हर (hara) – pluck out; पापचक्षुषाम् (pāpacakṣuṣām) – sinful eyes (pāpa:sinful | cakṣuṣām:eyes);

contentBlocks.original:
यन्नो भयं ग्रहेभ्योऽभूत्केतुभ्यो नृभ्य एव च ।
सरीसृपेभ्यो दंष्ट्रिभ्यो भूतेभ्योंऽहोभ्य एव च ॥ २७॥

सर्वाण्येतानि भगवन्नामरूपास्त्रकीर्तनात् ।
प्रयान्तु सङ्क्षयं सद्यो ये नः श्रेयःप्रतीपकाः ॥ २८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yanno bhayaṃ grahebhyo'bhūtketubhyo nṛbhya eva ca .
sarīsṛpebhyo daṃṣṭribhyo bhūtebhyoṃ'hobhya eva ca .. 27..

sarvāṇyetāni bhagavannāmarūpāstrakīrtanāt .
prayāntu saṅkṣayaṃ sadyo ye naḥ śreyaḥpratīpakāḥ .. 28..
contentBlocks.translation: May the glorification of the transcendental name, form, qualities and paraphernalia of the Supreme Personality of Godhead protect us from the influence of bad planets, meteors, envious human beings, serpents, scorpions, and animals like tigers and wolves. May it protect us from ghosts and the material elements like earth, water, fire and air, and may it also protect us from lightning and our past sins. We are always afraid of these hindrances to our auspicious life. Therefore, may they all be completely destroyed by the chanting of the Hare Krsna maha-mantra.
contentBlocks.meanings: यत् (yat) – which; नः (naḥ) – us; भयम् (bhayam) – fear; ग्रहेभ्यः (grahebhyaḥ) – from planets; अभूत् (abhūt) – became; केतुभ्यः (ketubhyaḥ) – from meteors; नृभ्यः (nṛbhyaḥ) – from humans; एव (eva) – indeed; च (ca) – and; सरीसृपेभ्यः (sarīsṛpebhyaḥ) – from reptiles; दंष्ट्रिभ्यः (daṃṣṭribhyaḥ) – from fanged beings; भूतेभ्यः (bhūtebhyaḥ) – from ghosts; अहोभ्यः (ahobhyaḥ) – from days; एव (eva) – indeed; च (ca) – and; सर्वाणि (sarvāṇi) – all; एतानि (etāni) – these; भगवत् (bhagavat) – of the Lord; नामरूपास्त्रकीर्तनात् (nāmarūpāstrakīrtanāt) – by glorifying name, form, weapon (nāma:name | rūpa:form | astra:weapon | kīrtanāt:by glorifying); प्रयान्तु (prayāntu) – may they go; सङ्क्षयम् (saṅkṣayam) – to destruction; सद्यः (sadyaḥ) – immediately; ये (ye) – those who; नः (naḥ) – our; श्रेयः (śreyaḥ) – auspiciousness; प्रतीपकाः (pratīpakāḥ) – obstacles;

contentBlocks.original:
गरुडो भगवान् स्तोत्रस्तोभश्छन्दोमयः प्रभुः ।
रक्षत्वशेषकृच्छ्रेभ्यो विष्वक्सेनः स्वनामभिः ॥ २९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
garuḍo bhagavān stotrastobhaśchandomayaḥ prabhuḥ .
rakṣatvaśeṣakṛcchrebhyo viṣvaksenaḥ svanāmabhiḥ .. 29 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Lord Garuda, the carrier of Lord Visnu, is the most worshipable lord, for he is as powerful as the Supreme Lord Himself. He is the personified Vedas and is worshiped by selected verses. May he protect us from all dangerous conditions, and may Lord Visvaksena, the Personality of Godhead, also protect us from all dangers by His holy names.
contentBlocks.meanings: गरुडः (garuḍaḥ) – Garuda; भगवान् (bhagavān) – the Lord; स्तोत्रस्तोभः (stotrastobhaḥ) – worshiped by hymns (stotra:hymn | stobhaḥ:praise); छन्दोमयः (chandomayaḥ) – personified Vedas (chandas:Vedas | mayaḥ:consisting of); प्रभुः (prabhuḥ) – the master; रक्षतु (rakṣatu) – may protect; अशेषकृच्छ्रेभ्यः (aśeṣakṛcchrebhyaḥ) – from all difficulties (aśeṣa:all | kṛcchrebhyaḥ:from difficulties); विष्वक्सेनः (viṣvaksenaḥ) – Visvaksena; स्वनामभिः (svanāmabhiḥ) – by His holy names (sva:own | nāmabhiḥ:by names);

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वापद्भ्यो हरेर्नामरूपयानायुधानि नः ।
बुद्धीन्द्रियमनःप्राणान्पान्तु पार्षदभूषणाः ॥ ३०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvāpadbhyo harernāmarūpayānāyudhāni naḥ .
buddhīndriyamanaḥprāṇānpāntu pārṣadabhūṣaṇāḥ .. 30..
contentBlocks.translation: May the Supreme Personality of Godhead's holy names, His transcendental forms, His carriers and all the weapons decorating Him as personal associates protect our intelligence, senses, mind and life air from all dangers.
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वापद्भ्यः (sarvāpadbhyaḥ) – from all dangers (sarva:all | apadbhyah:from dangers); हरेः (hareḥ) – of Hari; नामरूपयानायुधानि (nāmarūpayānāyudhāni) – names, forms, carriers, weapons (nāma:names | rūpa:forms | yāna:carriers | āyudhāni:weapons); नः (naḥ) – our; बुद्धीन्द्रियमनःप्राणान् (buddhīndriyamanaḥprāṇān) – intelligence, senses, mind, life air (buddhi:intelligence | indriya:senses | manaḥ:mind | prāṇān:life air); पान्तु (pāntu) – may protect; पार्षदभूषणाः (pārṣadabhūṣaṇāḥ) – personal associates as ornaments (pārṣada:associates | bhūṣaṇāḥ:ornaments);

contentBlocks.original:
यथा हि भगवानेव वस्तुतः सदसच्च यत् ।
सत्येनानेन नः सर्वे यान्तु नाशमुपद्रवाः ॥ ३१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā hi bhagavāneva vastutaḥ sadasacca yat .
satyenānena naḥ sarve yāntu nāśamupadravāḥ .. 31..
contentBlocks.translation: The subtle and gross cosmic manifestation is material, but nevertheless it is nondifferent from the Supreme Personality of Godhead because He is ultimately the cause of all causes. Cause and effect are factually one because the cause is present in the effect. Therefore the Absolute Truth, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, can destroy all our dangers by any of His potent parts.
contentBlocks.meanings: यथा (yathā) – just as; हि (hi) – indeed; भगवान् (bhagavān) – the Lord; एव (eva) – certainly; वस्तुतः (vastutaḥ) – in reality; सत् (sat) – existence; असत् (asat) – non-existence; च (ca) – and; यत् (yat) – which; सत्येन (satyena) – by truth; अनेन (anena) – by this; नः (naḥ) – our; सर्वे (sarve) – all; यान्तु (yāntu) – may go; नाशम् (nāśam) – destruction; उपद्रवाः (upadravāḥ) – dangers;

contentBlocks.original:
यथैकात्म्यानुभावानां विकल्परहितः स्वयम् ।
भूषणायुधलिङ्गाख्या धत्ते शक्तीः स्वमायया ॥ ३२॥

तेनैव सत्यमानेन सर्वज्ञो भगवान् हरिः ।
पातु सर्वैः स्वरूपैर्नः सदा सर्वत्र सर्वगः ॥ ३३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathaikātmyānubhāvānāṃ vikalparahitaḥ svayam .
bhūṣaṇāyudhaliṅgākhyā dhatte śaktīḥ svamāyayā .. 32..

tenaiva satyamānena sarvajño bhagavān hariḥ .
pātu sarvaiḥ svarūpairnaḥ sadā sarvatra sarvagaḥ .. 33..
contentBlocks.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead, the living entities, the material energy, the spiritual energy and the entire creation are all individual substances. In the ultimate analysis, however, together they constitute the supreme one, the Personality of Godhead. Therefore those who are advanced in spiritual knowledge see unity in diversity. For such advanced persons, the Lord's bodily decorations, His name, His fame, His attributes and forms and the weapons in His hand are manifestations of the strength of His potency. According to their elevated spiritual understanding, the omniscient Lord, who manifests various forms, is present everywhere. May He always protect us everywhere from all calamities.
contentBlocks.meanings: यथा (yathā) – as; एकात्म्य (ekātmyā) – unity; अनुभावानां (anubhāvānāṃ) – of experiences; विकल्परहितः (vikalparahitaḥ) – free from alternatives (vikalpa:alternatives | rahitaḥ:free from); स्वयम् (svayam) – himself; भूषणायुधलिङ्गाख्या (bhūṣaṇāyudhaliṅgākhyā) – known as ornaments, weapons, symbols (bhūṣaṇa:ornaments | āyudha:weapons | liṅga:symbols | ākhyā:known as); धत्ते (dhatte) – bears; शक्तीः (śaktīḥ) – powers; स्वमायया (svamāyayā) – by his own illusion (sva:own | māyayā:by illusion); तेनैव (tenaiva) – by that alone (tena:by that | eva:alone); सत्यमानेन (satyamānena) – by the true measure (satya:true | mānena:by measure); सर्वज्ञः (sarvajñaḥ) – omniscient; भगवान् (bhagavān) – the Lord; हरिः (hariḥ) – Hari; पातु (pātu) – may protect; सर्वैः (sarvaiḥ) – by all; स्वरूपैः (svarūpaiḥ) – by forms; नः (naḥ) – us; सदा (sadā) – always; सर्वत्र (sarvatra) – everywhere; सर्वगः (sarvagaḥ) – all-pervading;

contentBlocks.original:
विदिक्षु दिक्षूर्ध्वमधः समन्ता-
दन्तर्बहिर्भगवान्नारसिंहः ।
प्रहापयँलोकभयं स्वनेन
स्वतेजसा ग्रस्तसमस्ततेजाः ॥ ३४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidikṣu dikṣūrdhvamadhaḥ samantā-
dantarbahirbhagavānnārasiṃhaḥ .
prahāpayam̐lokabhayaṃ svanena
svatejasā grastasamastatejāḥ .. 34..
contentBlocks.translation: Prahlada Maharaja loudly chanted the holy name of Lord Narsimhadeva. May Lord Narsimhadeva, roaring for His devotee Prahlada Maharaja, protect us from all fear of dangers created by stalwart leaders in all directions through poison, weapons, water, fire, air and so on. May the Lord cover their influence by His own transcendental influence. May Narsimhadeva protect us in all directions and in all corners, above, below, within and without.
contentBlocks.meanings: विदिक्षु (vidikṣu) – in all directions (vi:all | dikṣu:in directions); दिक्षु (dikṣu) – in directions; ऊर्ध्वम् (ūrdhvam) – above; अधः (adhaḥ) – below; समन्तात् (samantāt) – all around; अन्तः (antaḥ) – within; बहि: (bahiḥ) – without; भगवान् (bhagavān) – the Lord; नारसिंहः (nārasiṃhaḥ) – Narasimha; प्रहापयँ (prahāpayam̐) – causing to roar; लोकभयम् (lokabhayam) – fear of the world (loka:world | bhayam:fear); स्वनेन (svanena) – by sound; स्वतेजसा (svatejasā) – by own brilliance (sva:own | tejasā:by brilliance); ग्रस्तसमस्ततेजाः (grastasamastatejāḥ) – absorbing all brilliance (grasta:absorbing | samasta:all | tejāḥ:brilliance);

contentBlocks.original:
मघवन्निदमाख्यातं वर्म नारायणात्मकम् ।
विजेष्यस्यञ्जसा येन दंशितोऽसुरयूथपान् ॥ ३५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maghavannidamākhyātaṃ varma nārāyaṇātmakam .
vijeṣyasyañjasā yena daṃśito'surayūthapān .. 35..
contentBlocks.translation: Visvarupa continued: O Indra, this mystic armor related to Lord Narayana has been described by me to you. By putting on this protective covering, you will certainly be able to conquer the leaders of the demons.
contentBlocks.meanings: मघवन् (maghavan) – O Indra; इदम् (idam) – this; आख्यातम् (ākhyātam) – described; वर्म (varma) – armor; नारायणात्मकम् (nārāyaṇātmakam) – related to Narayana (nārāyaṇa:Narayana | ātmakam:related to); विजेष्यसि (vijeṣyasi) – you will conquer; अञ्जसा (añjasā) – certainly; येन (yena) – by which; दंशितः (daṃśitaḥ) – protected; असुरयूथपान् (asurayūthapān) – leaders of the demons (asura:demon | yūthapān:leaders);

contentBlocks.original:
एतद्धारयमाणस्तु यं यं पश्यति चक्षुषा ।
पदा वा संस्पृशेत्सद्यः साध्वसात्स विमुच्यते ॥ ३६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
etaddhārayamāṇastu yaṃ yaṃ paśyati cakṣuṣā .
padā vā saṃspṛśetsadyaḥ sādhvasātsa vimucyate .. 36..
contentBlocks.translation: If one employs this armor, whomever he sees with his eyes or touches with his feet is immediately freed from all the above-mentioned dangers.
contentBlocks.meanings: एतत् (etat) – this; धारयमाणः (dhārayamāṇaḥ) – wearing; तु (tu) – but; यं (yaṃ) – whomever; यं (yaṃ) – whomever; पश्यति (paśyati) – sees; चक्षुषा (cakṣuṣā) – with eyes; पदा (padā) – with feet; वा (vā) – or; संस्पृशेत् (saṃspṛśet) – touches; सद्यः (sadyaḥ) – immediately; साध्वसात् (sādhvasāt) – from fear; स (sa) – he; विमुच्यते (vimucyate) – is freed;

contentBlocks.original:
न कुतश्चिद्भयं तस्य विद्यां धारयतो भवेत् ।
राजदस्युग्रहादिभ्यो व्याघ्रादिभ्यश्च कर्हिचित् ॥ ३७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na kutaścidbhayaṃ tasya vidyāṃ dhārayato bhavet .
rājadasyugrahādibhyo vyāghrādibhyaśca karhicit .. 37..
contentBlocks.translation: This prayer, Narayana-kavaca, constitutes subtle knowledge transcendentally connected with Narayana. One who employs this prayer is never disturbed or put in danger by the government, by plunderers, by evil demons or by any type of disease.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; कुतश्चित् (kutaścit) – from anywhere (kutaḥ:from where | cit:any); भयम् (bhayam) – fear; तस्य (tasya) – his; विद्याम् (vidyām) – knowledge; धारयतः (dhārayataḥ) – holding; भवेत् (bhavet) – would be; राजदस्युग्रहादिभ्यः (rājadasyugrahādibhyaḥ) – from kings, thieves, etc. (rāja:king | dasya:thief | graha:seizure | ādi:etc. | bhyaḥ:from); व्याघ्रादिभ्यः (vyāghrādibhyaḥ) – from tigers, etc. (vyāghra:tiger | ādi:etc. | bhyaḥ:from); च (ca) – and; कर्हिचित् (karhicit) – at any time;

contentBlocks.original:
इमां विद्यां पुरा कश्चित्कौशिको धारयन् द्विजः ।
योगधारणया स्वाङ्गं जहौ स मरुधन्वनि ॥ ३८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
imāṃ vidyāṃ purā kaścitkauśiko dhārayan dvijaḥ .
yogadhāraṇayā svāṅgaṃ jahau sa marudhanvani .. 38..
contentBlocks.translation: O King of heaven, a brahmana named Kausika formerly used this armor when he purposely gave up his body in the desert by mystic power.
contentBlocks.meanings: इमां (imāṃ) – this; विद्यां (vidyāṃ) – knowledge; पुरा (purā) – formerly; कश्चित् (kaścit) – some; कौशिकः (kauśikaḥ) – Kausika; धारयन् (dhārayan) – wearing; द्विजः (dvijaḥ) – brahmana; योगधारणया (yogadhāraṇayā) – by mystic power (yoga:mystic | dhāraṇayā:by power); स्वाङ्गं (svāṅgaṃ) – his body (sva:his | aṅgaṃ:body); जहौ (jahau) – gave up; स (sa) – he; मरुधन्वनि (marudhanvani) – in the desert (marut:desert | dhanvani:in);

contentBlocks.original:
तस्योपरि विमानेन गन्धर्वपतिरेकदा ।
ययौ चित्ररथः स्त्रीभिर्वृतो यत्र द्विजक्षयः ॥ ३९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasyopari vimānena gandharvapatirekadā .
yayau citrarathaḥ strībhirvṛto yatra dvijakṣayaḥ .. 39 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Surrounded by many beautiful women, Citraratha, the King of Gandharvaloka, was once passing in his airplane over the brahmana's body at the spot where the brahmana had died.
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्य (tasya) – his; उपरि (upari) – above; विमानेन (vimānena) – by the airplane; गन्धर्वपतिः (gandharvapatiḥ) – King of Gandharvas (gandharva:Gandharvas | patiḥ:king); एकदा (ekadā) – once; ययौ (yayau) – went; चित्ररथः (citrarathaḥ) – Citraratha; स्त्रीभिः (strībhiḥ) – with women; वृतः (vṛtaḥ) – surrounded; यत्र (yatra) – where; द्विजक्षयः (dvijakṣayaḥ) – brahmana's death (dvija:brahmana | kṣayaḥ:death);

contentBlocks.original:
गगनान्न्यपतत्सद्यः सविमानो ह्यवाक्शिराः ।
स वालखिल्यवचनादस्थीन्यादाय विस्मितः ।
प्रास्य प्राचीसरस्वत्यां स्नात्वा धाम स्वमन्वगात् ॥ ४०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gaganān nyapatatsadyaḥ savimāno hyavākśirāḥ .
sa vālakhilyavacanādasthīnyādāya vismitaḥ .
prāsya prācīsarasvatyāṃ snātvā dhāma svamanvagāt .. 40..
contentBlocks.translation: Suddenly Citraratha was forced to fall from the sky headfirst with his airplane. Struck with wonder, he was ordered by the great sages named the Valikhilyas to throw the brahmana's bones in the nearby River Sarasvati. He had to do this and bathe in the river before returning to his own abode.
contentBlocks.meanings: गगनात् (gaganāt) – from the sky; न्यपतत् (nyapatat) – fell; सद्यः (sadyaḥ) – suddenly; सविमानः (savimānaḥ) – with his airplane (sa:with | vimānaḥ:airplane); ह्यवाक्शिराः (hyavākśirāḥ) – headfirst (hi:indeed | avāk:downward | śirāḥ:head); स (sa) – he; वालखिल्यवचनात् (vālakhilyavacanāt) – by the words of Valikhilyas (vālakhilya:Valikhilyas | vacanāt:by the words); अस्थीनि (asthīni) – bones; आदाय (ādāya) – taking; विस्मितः (vismitaḥ) – astonished; प्रास्य (prāsya) – throwing; प्राचीसरस्वत्याम् (prācīsarasvatyām) – in the eastern Sarasvati (prācī:eastern | sarasvatyām:in the Sarasvati); स्नात्वा (snātvā) – bathing; धाम (dhāma) – abode; स्वम् (svam) – his own; अन्वगात् (anvagāt) – returned;

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीशुक उवाच ।
य इदं श‍ृणुयात्काले यो धारयति चादृतः ।
तं नमस्यन्ति भूतानि मुच्यते सर्वतो भयात् ॥ ४१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśuka uvāca .
ya idaṃ śṛṇuyātkāle yo dhārayati cādṛtaḥ .
taṃ namasyanti bhūtāni mucyate sarvato bhayāt .. 41..
contentBlocks.translation: Sri Sukadeva said: My dear Maharaja Pariksit, one who employs this armor or hears about it with faith and veneration when afraid because of any conditions in the material world is immediately freed from all dangers and is worshiped by all living entities.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रीशुक (śrīśuka) – Sri Sukadeva (śrī:Sri | śuka:Sukadeva); उवाच (uvāca) – said; यः (yaḥ) – who; इदं (idaṃ) – this; शृणुयात् (śṛṇuyāt) – hears; काले (kāle) – in time; यः (yaḥ) – who; धारयति (dhārayati) – employs; च (ca) – and; आदृतः (ādṛtaḥ) – with veneration; तम् (tam) – him; नमस्यन्ति (namasyanti) – worship; भूतानि (bhūtāni) – living entities; मुच्यते (mucyate) – is freed; सर्वतः (sarvataḥ) – from all; भयात् (bhayāt) – dangers;

contentBlocks.original:
एतां विद्यामधिगतो विश्वरूपाच्छतक्रतुः ।
त्रैलोक्यलक्ष्मीं बुभुजे विनिर्जित्य मृधेऽसुरान् ॥ ४२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
etāṃ vidyāmadhigato viśvarūpācchatakratuḥ .
trailokyalakṣmīṃ bubhuje vinirjitya mṛdhe'surān .. 42..
contentBlocks.translation: King Indra, who performed one hundred sacrifices, received this prayer of protection from Visvarupa. After conquering the demons, he enjoyed all the opulences of the three worlds.
contentBlocks.meanings: एताम् (etām) – this; विद्याम् (vidyām) – knowledge; अधिगतः (adhigataḥ) – obtained; विश्वरूपात् (viśvarūpāt) – from Viśvarūpa; शतक्रतुः (śatakratuḥ) – Indra; त्रैलोक्यलक्ष्मीम् (trailokyalakṣmīm) – opulences of the three worlds (trai:three | lokya:worlds | lakṣmīm:opulences); बुभुजे (bubhuje) – enjoyed; विनिर्जित्य (vinirjitya) – conquering; मृधे (mṛdhe) – in battle; असुरान् (asurān) – demons;

contentBlocks.original:
इति श्रीमद्भागवत महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां संहितायां
षष्ठस्कन्धे नारायणवर्म कथनं नामाष्टमोऽध्यायः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti śrīmadbhāgavata mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ
ṣaṣṭhaskandhe nārāyaṇavarma kathanaṃ nāmāṣṭamo'dhyāyaḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Thus, in the Śrīmad Bhāgavata Mahāpurāṇa, in the section for the supreme renunciates,
In the sixth book, the eighth chapter is named "The Story of Narayana’s Armor."
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; श्रीमद्भागवत (śrīmadbhāgavata) – glorious Bhāgavata (śrīmat:glorious | bhāgavata:Bhāgavata); महापुराणे (mahāpurāṇe) – in the great Purāṇa (mahā:great | purāṇe:in the Purāṇa); पारमहंस्यां (pāramahaṃsyāṃ) – for the supreme renunciates (pāra:supreme | mahaṃsyāṃ:for the renunciates); संहितायां (saṃhitāyāṃ) – in the section; षष्ठस्कन्धे (ṣaṣṭhaskandhe) – in the sixth book (ṣaṣṭha:sixth | skandhe:in the book); नारायणवर्म (nārāyaṇavarma) – Narayana’s Armor (nārāyaṇa:Narayana | varma:armor); कथनं (kathanaṃ) – story; नाम (nāma) – named; अष्टमः (aṣṭamaḥ) – eighth; अध्यायः (adhyāyaḥ) – chapter;
